name: High-Value Card Usage
description: Alert when a single card transaction exceeds $5,000. High-value transactions are inherently riskier and warrant additional scrutiny.
recommendation: Verify cardholder authorization for high-value transaction. Check merchant category and geographic location against card holder profile.

name: Rapid Multi-Transaction Pattern
description: Detect when the same card is used for 3+ transactions within a 1-hour window. This pattern can indicate card cloning or testing.
recommendation: Review all transactions in the burst. Rapid-fire usage across different merchants is a strong fraud indicator. Consider card block/replacement.

name: Merchant Mismatch Variation
description: Flag cases where an anomalous transaction is followed by different merchant usage than typical pattern. Suggests account compromise.
recommendation: Compare merchants against historical card usage. If significantly different, contact cardholder to verify. High probability of unauthorized use.

name: Card Testing Pattern
description: Identify sequences of small transactions ($1-$20) in quick succession. Classic card testing attack: attacker validates stolen card with micro-charges.
recommendation: This is a strong fraud indicator. Recommend immediate card block and cardholder notification. Review all recent transactions for unauthorized use.

name: High Z-Score Anomaly
description: Transactions with z-score > 5.0 indicate extreme deviation from baseline (>5 standard deviations). Very rare and high-risk.
recommendation: Extreme anomaly detected. Strong probability of fraud. Contact cardholder immediately for verification. Prepare for escalation if unauthorized.

name: Geographic Anomaly
description: Transaction from a location significantly distant from cardholder's typical usage area (e.g., international after domestic-only history).
recommendation: Verify geographic plausibility. If cardholder was traveling, expected pattern. If not, high fraud risk. Check merchant and transaction details.
